en.extract:
Thekla's lark (Galerida theklae), also known as the Thekla lark, is a species of lark that breeds on the Iberian Peninsula and in North and East Africa south to Kenya. It is a sedentary (non-migratory) species. This is a common bird of dry open country, often at some altitude. Thekla's lark was named by Alfred Edmund Brehm in 1857 for his recently deceased sister Thekla Brehm (1833–1857). The name is a modern Greek one, Θέκλα (Thekla), which comes from ancient Greek Θεόκλεια (Theokleia) derived from θεός (theos, "god") and κλέος (kleos, "glory" or "honour"). The population is declining in parts of Spain, but this is a common bird with a very wide range and the International Union for Conservation of Nature has rated its conservation status as being of "least concern".


Taxonomy and systematics
Thekla's lark has several East African subspecies that show deep genetic divergence both among themselves and from the Mediterranean populations and are consequently strong candidates for consideration as separate species. Formerly, some authorities considered Thekla's lark and the Malabar lark to be conspecific. Alternate names for Thekla's lark include short-crested lark and Thekla crested-lark.


Subspecies
Twelve subspecies are recognized: 

G. t. theklae - Brehm, AE, 1857: Also known as Iberian Thekla's lark. Found in Portugal, Spain, Balearic Islands and extreme southern France
North Moroccan Thekla's lark (G. t. erlangeri) - Hartert, 1904: Found in northern Morocco
Central Moroccan Thekla's lark (G. t. ruficolor) - Whitaker, 1898: Found in central and north-eastern Morocco, northern Algeria and northern Tunisia
G. t. theresae - Meinertzhagen, R, 1939: Found in south-western Morocco and Mauritania
Hauts Plateaux Thekla's lark (G. t. superflua) - Hartert, 1897: Found in eastern Morocco, northern Algeria and eastern Tunisia
North African Thekla's lark (G. t. carolinae) - Erlanger, 1897: Found in eastern Morocco through the northern Sahara to north-western Egypt
G. t. harrarensis - Érard & Jarry, 1973: Found in eastern Ethiopia
G. t. huei - Érard & Naurois, 1973: Found in south-central Ethiopia
Abyssinian Thekla's lark (G. t. praetermissa) - (Blanford, 1869): Found in southern Eritrea to central Ethiopia
Somali Thekla's lark (G. t. ellioti) - Hartert, 1897: Originally described as a separate species. Found in northern and central Somalia
G. t. mallablensis - Colston, 1982: Found in southern Somalia
G. t. huriensis - Benson, 1947: Found in southern Ethiopia and northern Kenya


Description
This is a smallish lark, slightly smaller than the Eurasian skylark. It has a long, spiky, erectile crest. Its colouring is similar to the widespread crested lark (G. cristata), being predominantly brownish-grey or brown, although its back and rump are greyer than that species. Its breast is streaked with black. It is smaller and somewhat greyer than the crested lark, and has a shorter bill. In flight, it shows grey underwings, whereas the crested lark has reddish underwings. It is also greyer than the Eurasian skylark, and lacks the white wing and tail edge of that species. The body is mainly dark-streaked grey above and whitish below. The sexes are similar.


Distribution and habitat
Thekla's lark is native and resident in France, Spain, Portugal, Algeria, Egypt, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Libya, Morocco, Somalia, Tunisia and Western Sahara. Its typical habitat is rugged areas with scrub, bare patches of ground and semi-arid grassland.


Behaviour and ecology

It nests on the ground, laying two to six eggs. Its food is weed, seeds and insects, the last being especially popular in the breeding season.
The song is melodious and varied, with mournful whistles and mimicry included. It is softer and more tuneful than that of the crested lark, and may be sung during flight or from the ground or an exposed perch.


References


External links

Videos, photos and sounds – Internet Bird Collection
Ageing and sexing (PDF; 2.3 MB) by Javier Blasco-Zumeta & Gerd-Michael Heinze
Distinguishing characters between Thekla lark and crested lark (PDF; 1.7 MB)

in the desert South East of [[Douz]], Tunisia | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = {{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International. |year=2024 |title=''Galerida theklae'' |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22717387A264571232 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22717387A264571232.en |access-date=28 February 2026}} | taxon = Galerida theklae | authority = [[Alfred Edmund Brehm|Brehm, AE]], 1857 | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | synonyms = * ''Galerita Theklae'' * ''Galerida malabarica theklae'' | range_map = Galerida theklae distribution map.png }} '''Thekla's lark''' ('''''Galerida theklae'''''), also known as the '''Thekla lark''', is a species of [[lark]] that breeds on the [[Iberian Peninsula]] and in North and East Africa south to Kenya. It is a sedentary ([[Bird migration|non-migratory]]) species. This is a common bird of dry open country, often at some altitude. Thekla's lark was named by [[Alfred Edmund Brehm]] in 1857 for his recently deceased sister Thekla Brehm (1833–1857). The name is a modern Greek one, {{Lang|el|Θέκλα}} ([[wiktionary:Thekla|Thekla]]), which comes from ancient Greek {{Lang|grc|Θεόκλεια}} (Theokleia) derived from [[wiktionary:θεός|θεός]] ([[wiktionary:theos|''theos'']], "god") and [[wiktionary:κλέος|κλέος]] ({{Lang|grc-Latn|kleos}}, "glory" or "honour").[http://www.behindthename.com/name/thekla ''Thekla'']. Behind the Name, retrieved 16-01-2013. Brehm later had five children, four of who died of diphtheria in 1883, including a daughter also named Thekla. The population is declining in parts of Spain, but this is a common bird with a very wide range and the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature]] has rated its conservation status as being of "[[Least-concern species|least concern]]". ==Taxonomy and systematics== Thekla's lark has several East African subspecies that show deep genetic divergence both among themselves and from the Mediterranean populations and are consequently strong candidates for consideration as separate species.{{Cite journal|last=Alström|first=Per|last2=Barnes|first2=Keith N.|last3=Olsson|first3=Urban|last4=Barker|first4=F. Keith|last5=Bloomer|first5=Paulette|last6=Khan|first6=Aleem Ahmed|last7=Qureshi|first7=Masood Ahmed|last8=Guillaumet|first8=Alban|last9=Crochet|first9=Pierre-André|date=2013-12-01|title=Multilocus phylogeny of the avian family Alaudidae (larks) reveals complex morphological evolution, non-monophyletic genera and hidden species diversity|journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution|volume=69|issue=3|pages=1043–1056|doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2013.06.005|issn=1095-9513|pmid=23792153|url=https://pub.epsilon.slu.se/11222/2/alstrom_fig_1.pdf}} Formerly, some authorities considered Thekla's lark and the [[Malabar lark]] to be [[conspecific]]. Alternate names for Thekla's lark include '''short-crested lark''' and '''Thekla crested-lark'''.{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=5351025CD2FE0285|title=Galerida theklae - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-12-10}} === Subspecies === Twelve [[subspecies]] are recognized:{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.4|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.4|doi-access=free}} * ''G. t. theklae'' - [[Alfred Edmund Brehm|Brehm, AE]], 1857: Also known as '''Iberian Thekla's lark'''. Found in [[Portugal]], Spain, [[Balearic Islands]] and extreme [[southern France]] * '''North Moroccan Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. erlangeri'') - [[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1904: Found in northern [[Morocco]] * '''Central Moroccan Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. ruficolor'') - [[Joseph Whitaker (ornithologist)|Whitaker]], 1898: Found in central and north-eastern Morocco, northern [[Algeria]] and northern [[Tunisia]] * ''G. t. theresae'' - [[Richard Meinertzhagen|Meinertzhagen, R]], 1939: Found in south-western Morocco and [[Mauritania]] * '''Hauts Plateaux Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. superflua'') - Hartert, 1897: Found in eastern Morocco, northern Algeria and eastern Tunisia * '''North African Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. carolinae'') - [[Carlo von Erlanger|Erlanger]], 1897: Found in eastern Morocco through the northern [[Sahara]] to north-western [[Egypt]] * ''G. t. harrarensis'' - [[Christian Érard|Érard]] & [[Guy Jarry|Jarry]], 1973: Found in eastern [[Ethiopia]] * ''G. t. huei'' - Érard & [[René de Naurois|Naurois]], 1973: Found in south-central Ethiopia * '''Abyssinian Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. praetermissa'') - ([[William Thomas Blanford|Blanford]], 1869): Found in southern [[Eritrea]] to central Ethiopia * '''Somali Thekla's lark''' (''G. t. ellioti'') - Hartert, 1897: Originally described as a separate species. Found in northern and central Somalia * ''G. t. mallablensis'' - Colston, 1982: Found in southern Somalia * ''G. t. huriensis'' - [[Constantine Walter Benson|Benson]], 1947: Found in southern Ethiopia and northern [[Kenya]] Thekla's lark (Galerida theklae theklae) Loja.jpg|''G. t. theklae''
in hills near [[Loja, Granada]], Spain Thekla lark (Galerida theklae ruficolor).jpg|''G. t. ruficolor''
[[Souss-Massa National Park]], Morocco ==Description== This is a smallish [[lark]], slightly smaller than the [[Eurasian skylark]]. It has a long, spiky, erectile [[Crest (feathers)|crest]]. Its colouring is similar to the widespread [[crested lark]] (''G. cristata''), being predominantly brownish-grey or brown, although its back and rump are greyer than that species. Its breast is streaked with black. It is smaller and somewhat greyer than the crested lark, and has a shorter bill. In flight, it shows grey underwings, whereas the crested lark has reddish underwings.{{cite book |url=https://www.google.co.uk/books/edition/Flight_Identification_of_European_Passer/tDf_DwAAQBAJ?hl=en&pg=PA196 |page=196 |title=Flight Identification of European Passerines and Select Landbirds: An Illustrated and Photographic Guide |author=Tomasz Cofta |isbn=9780691177571 |year=2021 |publisher=Princeton University Press}} It is also greyer than the Eurasian skylark, and lacks the white wing and tail edge of that species. The body is mainly dark-streaked grey above and whitish below. The sexes are similar.{{cite book|author1=Mark Beaman|author2=Steve Madge|title=The Handbook of Bird Identification: For Europe and the Western Palearctic|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p968cQkfSroC&pg=PA549 |year=1998 |publisher=Christopher Helm |isbn=978-0-7136-3960-5 |page=549}} ==Distribution and habitat== Thekla's lark is native and resident in France, Spain, Portugal, Algeria, Egypt, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, [[Libya]], Morocco, Somalia, Tunisia and [[Western Sahara]]. Its typical habitat is rugged areas with scrub, bare patches of ground and semi-arid grassland. ==Behaviour and ecology== [[File:Galerida theklae MHNT 232 Laghouat Algérie.jpg|thumb|Eggs of ''Galerida theklae'' [[MHNT]]]] It nests on the ground, laying two to six [[bird egg|eggs]]. Its food is weed, seeds and insects, the last being especially popular in the breeding season.{{cite web | url = http://www.hbw.com/node/57675 | title = Thekla Lark (''Galerida theklae'')| access-date = 17 November 2016 | publisher = Lynx Edicions|work = Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive}} The song is melodious and varied, with mournful whistles and mimicry included. It is softer and more tuneful than that of the crested lark, and may be sung during flight or from the ground or an exposed perch. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Galerida theklae}} {{Wikispecies|Galerida theklae}} * [https://search.macaulaylibrary.org/catalog?taxonCode=thelar1 Videos, photos and sounds] – Internet Bird Collection *[https://web.archive.org/web/20141202064202/http://aulaenred.ibercaja.es/wp-content/uploads/300_TheklaLarkGtheklae.pdf Ageing and sexing (PDF; 2.3 MB) by Javier Blasco-Zumeta & Gerd-Michael Heinze] * [https://web.archive.org/web/20141202065715/http://aulaenred.ibercaja.es/wp-content/uploads/300a_IdentificationG_cristata-G_theklae.pdf Distinguishing characters between Thekla lark and crested lark (PDF; 1.7 MB)] {{Taxonbar|from=Q851570}} [[Category:Galerida|Thekla lark]] [[Category:Birds of North Africa]] [[Category:Birds of East Africa]] [[Category:Birds of Europe]] [[Category:Birds described in 1857|Thekla lark]] [[Category:Taxa named by Alfred Brehm|Thekla lark]]

zh.extract:
短嘴凤头百灵（学名：Galerida theklae）是百灵科凤头百灵属的一种，分布于法国、直布罗陀、埃及、利比亚、阿尔及利亚、西撒哈拉、埃塞俄比亚、突尼斯、肯尼亚、厄立特里亚、西班牙、索马里、摩洛哥和葡萄牙。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
短嘴凤头百灵的平均体重约为39.8克。栖息地包括温带草原、亚热带或热带的（低地）干燥疏灌丛、牧草地、耕地、地中海型疏灌丛和亚热带或热带的（低地）干草原。


参考文献

Galerida theklae. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27].
